author: claude-code-agent

patch.value:
The MCP per-action parameter contracts (`PM_TOOL_ACTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS` in
`src/sdk/cli-contracts/tool-schema.ts`) have drifted from the CLI flag tables in
`flag-contracts.ts`: several flags the CLI advertises are missing from the
action's required/optional key set, so the strict MCP/SDK schema rejects options
the CLI still accepts. Surfaced by CodeRabbit during PR #396 (cli-contracts
decomposition); the drift is pre-existing (the contracts were relocated verbatim,
not introduced by the split).

## Confirmed missing (CLI flag present, MCP action key absent)

- `guide.list` — `--list`. Property `list` exists but its metadata description is
  files/docs-specific ("list the current linked paths"); needs a broadened or
  action-scoped description so the guide schema reads correctly.
- `health.brief` — `--brief`. Property `brief` exists (sibling of the already
  exposed `summary`); add to the `health` optional list.
- `validate.parentCycleSeverity` — `--parent-cycle-severity`. Sibling of the
  already exposed `dependencyCycleSeverity`, but has NO property definition in
  `tool-parameter-tables.ts` — add a `{ type: "string", enum: ["off","warn","error"] }`
  property + metadata mirroring `dependencyCycleSeverity`, then add to `validate`
  optional.
- `contracts.full` — `--full`. Property `full` exists; add to the `contracts`
  optional list.

## Why deferred from PR #396

PR #396 is a behavior-preserving decomposition (byte-identical `full.json`
snapshot). These fixes change the MCP contract + regenerate `full.json`, and
require per-param metadata judgment (guide.list description; new
parentCycleSeverity property). They belong in a focused contract-parity PR.

## Scope / acceptance

- Audit ALL action contracts vs their `*_FLAG_CONTRACTS` for the same
  sibling-omission pattern (only expose flags meant for MCP — exclude
  interactive-only flags like `--stream`/`--watch`/`--progress`).
- Add the missing property defs + metadata; regenerate `tests/fixtures/contracts/full.json`.
- Add a parity test asserting each MCP action contract key set is a superset of
  its non-interactive CLI flags so the drift cannot recur.
MCP action-schema contracts drifted from CLI flag tables (guide.list, health.brief, validate.parentCycleSeverity, contracts.full)

message: Track pre-existing MCP/CLI action-schema drift surfaced in PR #396
